title: Churchill Downs Incorporated Announces State of Maryland's Decision to Acquire the Preakness IP Rights
author: Globe Newswire
description: Churchill Downs Incorporated announced that the State of Maryland has decided to exercise its rights to acquire the intellectual property rights of the Preakness Stakes and Black-Eyed Susan Stakes from 1/ST Maryland LLC by matching CDI's previously-announced $85 million purchase agreement. CDI's CEO stated the company remains committed to working with Maryland leadership on redeveloping Pimlico and the Preakness Stakes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company lost its $85 million acquisition deal for the Preakness IP rights as Maryland exercised its right to match the purchase price and acquire the assets instead. This represents a significant loss of a strategic asset and expansion opportunity for CDI.

title: Churchill Downs Incorporated Announces State of Maryland’s Decision to Acquire the Preakness IP Rights
author: Na
description: Churchill Downs Incorporated announced that the State of Maryland has exercised its right to acquire the intellectual property rights of the Preakness Stakes and Black-Eyed Susan Stakes for $85 million, matching CDI's previously announced purchase agreement. CDI's CEO stated the company remains committed to working with Maryland leadership on redeveloping Pimlico and the Preakness Stakes.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: CDI lost a significant $85 million acquisition deal for the Preakness IP rights due to Maryland's state right of first refusal. This represents a loss of a major asset and strategic opportunity, though management's measured response suggests acceptance of the outcome.

title: Golden Tempo Wins the 152nd Running of the Kentucky Derby Presented by Woodford Reserve
author: Churchill Downs Incorporated
description: Golden Tempo won the 152nd Kentucky Derby at 23-1 odds under trainer Cherie DeVaux, who became the first woman to train a Derby winner. The event drew over 150,000 fans and generated record Derby Week wagering of $487 million across all sources, up 3% from 2025. Churchill Downs expects record Adjusted EBITDA of $15-18 million growth for Derby Week.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company reported record-breaking Derby Week wagering of $487 million (up 3% YoY), record TwinSpires handle of $129 million, and expects record Adjusted EBITDA growth of $15-18 million. Strong operational performance and attendance (150,000+ fans) indicate robust business momentum.

title: Always A Runner Claims the Lilies for the 152nd Running of the Longines Kentucky Oaks
author: Churchill Downs Incorporated
description: Churchill Downs Incorporated reported record-breaking results for the 152nd Kentucky Oaks, with Always A Runner winning the race. The event attracted over 103,000 fans and generated $89 million in total wagering (up 18% year-over-year) and $29 million on the Kentucky Oaks race alone (up 29% year-over-year). NBC Sports broadcast the event in primetime for the first time, and TwinSpires set a new record of $24 million in wagering.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong financial performance with record-breaking wagering across all metrics (18% increase in total card wagering, 29% increase in Kentucky Oaks race wagering, 24% increase in TwinSpires handle). Expanded media reach through primetime NBC Sports broadcast, increased attendance (103,000+ fans), and successful execution of the 152nd Kentucky Oaks event demonstrate operational excellence and growing audience engagement.

title: Churchill Downs Incorporated Announces Definitive Agreement to Acquire Preakness Intellectual Property Rights
author: Na
description: Churchill Downs Incorporated (NASDAQ: CHDN) has agreed to acquire the intellectual property rights of the Preakness Stakes and Black-Eyed Susan Stakes from 1/ST Maryland LLC for $85 million. The transaction, expected to close after the 2026 Preakness Stakes, will be funded through cash on hand and existing credit facilities. CDI will license the IP to the State of Maryland to conduct the races annually.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The acquisition of iconic Preakness Stakes IP represents strategic expansion into premier thoroughbred racing assets with long-term growth potential. CEO commentary emphasizes alignment with company strategy and support for venue redevelopment, indicating confidence in the investment's value creation.
